raw_consumer_statement:
I am re-filing a complaint regarding XXXX handling of a dispute involving a {$25000.00} transaction that resulted from a documented business email compromise ( XXXX ) fraud. 

Since my prior complaint, Citi has completed its investigation and issued a final determination stating that I am responsible because the services were provided. 

I believe this conclusion reflects an overly narrow and incomplete evaluation of the transaction and does not appropriately consider the impact of criminal impersonation on the validity of the authorization.

This transaction was initiated as the direct result of a confirmed business email compromise in which a third party impersonated my contractor and provided fraudulent payment instructions. Law enforcement has confirmed that this was part of a coordinated fraud scheme. 

While Citi has focused on the fact that the payment processor ( XXXX ) executed the transaction as instructed, this does not address the core issue : the authorization itself was obtained through criminal impersonation, and the intended recipient ( my XXXX ) never received the funds. 

In effect, Citi evaluated whether the payment processor performed its function, but did not meaningfully evaluate whether the underlying transaction was valid or fulfilled its intended purpose. 

I am requesting that Citi reconsider this matter using a more complete analysis that accounts for documented impersonation fraud and the failure of the intended transaction outcome. 
I have already escalated this matter to Citis Executive Response Unit and am seeking regulatory review of Citis handling of this dispute.
